unresolved_items: reviewer-codex:group-1: Terminal-state validation is still asymmetric: `frozen` and `frozen_with_conditions` are checked against remaining material work, but `pushback` is itself treated as proof of materiality, so minor-only end states can be escalated into `resume_required`.

reviewer-codex:group-2: The mirrored Codex invoker still routes prompt text through `bash` command-line transport, which violates the shared implementor rulebook and makes live Windows execution depend on an unvalidated shell path.
reviewer-codex:group-3: The shared-module loader is not a true source/dist fallback, so live JS consumers can fail before reaching the intended canonical shared runtime.
improvements_applied: The final-round ultimatum branch now updates the round snapshot and canonical round compliance/fix evidence before terminal closeout, and PM-003 is now restricted to the canonical four-count shape across run and cycle postmortems.
Run-postmortem and cycle-postmortem now read the same post-ultimatum self-check source because the ultimatum branch updates roundResult.selfCheckIssues before closeout.
Cycle review_issue_count now uses the same recognized-severity filtering as the board-side postmortem writer, eliminating the latent counting divergence.
